select_when: A small subset of causes, customers, tasks, or constraints appears to drive a disproportionate share of the result.
Time or attention is scarce enough that the main problem is deciding what not to work on.
A complex issue tree needs pruning so analysis concentrates on the branches most likely to move the answer.
The team can revisit the prioritization as the distribution shifts instead of defending the current vital few as permanent truth.
avoid_when: The 80/20 framing is being used as rhetoric without actual leverage measurement, making it a lazy excuse to ignore inconvenient work.
Emerging drivers or long-horizon bets are still too early to measure cleanly, so aggressive pruning would likely discard future-important factors.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model focused on uneven contribution, pruning, and revisitable prioritization instead of turning 80/20 into a universal law.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor general productivity rhetoric because the raw source is stronger on vital-few selection and anti-premature-pruning discipline.
open_questions: The source spans customer strategy, policy trade-offs, and learning, so the typed output compresses several application registers.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on highly uneven contribution, scarce attention, issue-tree pruning, and revisitable leverage points.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions come directly from the source warnings about lazy 80/20 rhetoric and neglect of emerging opportunities.

input_type.note: The source activates around uneven distributions and prioritization pressure; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source naturally yields a vital-few focus plan, but the typed output label is a reviewed operational compression.

reasoning_types.note: Pareto uses causal leverage logic and second-order awareness of pruning trade-offs, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
